error.parenOpen: The parenthesis `(` was never closed. Add `)` to close it
error.bracketOpen: The bracket `[` was never closed. Add `]` to close it
error.braceOpen: The brace `{` was never closed. Add `}` to close it
error.eofParsing: A closing `)` or `]` is missing. Close the bracket you opened
error.unterminatedString: The `'` or `"` mark was never closed. Add one at the end too
error.eolString: The `'` or `"` mark was never closed. Add one at the end too
error.fullwidthChar: There's a full-width character mixed in. Switch your keyboard to English mode
error.invalidChar: There's a character that can't be used. Delete it and try again
error.expectedColon: A `:` is missing. Put `:` at the end of `if` or `for`
error.unmatchedParen: Too many closing `)`. Check the number of brackets
error.invalidSyntax: Something is written wrong. Check the spelling and symbols
error.nameNotDefined: What is `{{name}}`? You haven't created it yet, or check the spelling
error.strConcat: You can't join text and numbers directly. Try wrapping it with `str()`
error.unsupportedOperand: You're trying to mix text and numbers in a calculation. Use `int()` or `str()` to match them
error.takesArgs: `{{fn}}()` needs {{expected}} things but you gave it {{given}}
error.unexpectedIndent: Too many spaces. Line up with the line above
error.expectedIndent: Not enough spaces. Add 4 spaces after `if` or `for`
error.indexOutOfRange: The list number is too big. Check the length with `len()`
error.divisionByZero: You can't divide by zero. Check the number you're dividing by
error.invalidInt: What's inside `int()` isn't a number. Only put numbers in it
error.invalidFloat: What's inside `float()` isn't a number. Only put numbers in it
error.noAttribute: `{{type}}` doesn't have a feature called `{{attr}}`. Check the name
error.keyError: That name isn't in the dictionary. Use `print()` to check what's inside

lp.title: Python Notebook — Browser-based Python for Kids
lp.description: No installation needed. A browser-based Python learning environment for kids. Native language UI, kid-friendly error messages, auto-save. Works on Chromebook.
lp.ogDescription: Python runs right in your browser. A programming notebook for kids
lp.heroCatch:
Python runs right in your browser.
A programming notebook for kids
lp.heroSub: No installation. Kid-friendly errors. In your language.
lp.problem1: 😢 Tried to install Python, but it was too complicated and gave up
lp.problem2: 💻 Can't install software on a Chromebook
lp.problem3: 🌍 Online Python tools are all in English — too hard for kids
lp.problem4: ❓ Error messages are in English — no idea what to fix
lp.feature1Desc: Just open your browser. Works on Chromebooks and tablets too.
lp.feature2Desc: Buttons and menus in your language. Easy for young learners.
lp.feature3Desc: English errors translated into words kids understand. Shows how to fix them too.
lp.feature4Desc: Auto-save. Close the browser and your code is still there.
lp.errorDemoIntro: English error messages are translated into words kids can understand. It also shows how to fix them.
lp.errorTranslated1: The parenthesis ( was never closed. Add ) to close it
lp.errorTranslated2: What is hensuu? You haven't created it yet, or check the spelling
lp.errorTranslated3: Not enough spaces. Add 4 spaces after if or for
lp.scratchP1: You learned to make programs by putting blocks together in Scratch. Amazing!
lp.scratchP2: But if you want to write programs with text, the next step is Python.
lp.scratchP3: With Python Notebook, you can write the same "repeat" and "if" blocks from Scratch — but in Python.
lp.scratchP4: The app's "Examples" section has Python code that does the same things as Scratch blocks.
lp.example1Code:
for i in range(5):
    print("★" * i)
lp.safety1Desc: All data is processed in your child's browser. Nothing is sent to external servers.
lp.safety2Desc: Uses WebAssembly (Pyodide) to run standard Python 3.
lp.safety3Desc: Learns standard Python syntax, so knowledge transfers when moving to a full environment.
lp.step1Alt: Screen showing the app opened in a browser
lp.step2Alt: Screen showing Python code being typed
lp.step3Desc: Press the button and see the result.
lp.shareText: Python runs right in your browser! A programming notebook for kids

privacy.title: Privacy Policy — Python Notebook
privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.dataDesc: All code and execution results in Python Notebook are processed entirely within your browser. No data is ever sent to external servers.
privacy.storageDesc: We use your browser's local storage for the following purposes:
privacy.storageItem1: Auto-saving your code (so it's still there when you come back)
privacy.storageItem2: Remembering whether you've seen the bookmark reminder
privacy.storageItem3: Saving your language preference (so it remembers your chosen language)
privacy.analyticsDesc: We currently do not use any analytics tools. If we add them in the future, we'll update this page.
